content: Just as human linguistic reflexion (the common vocabulary term for intentional critical reflection taught in high school literacy curricula) is a deliberate post-hoc practice that improves writing and reasoning, current LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be improved by explicitly grounding their iterative reflection routines in structured, literacy-style reflexion frameworks. This frames LLM reflection not as a novel engineered trick, but as a computational instantiation of the foundational metacognitive reflexion skill humans have taught for centuries to boost cognitive performance.
